data.title: Marti Technologies’ Earnings Call Signals Profitable Pivot
data.description:
Marti Technologies Inc ((MRT)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Marti Technologies’ latest earnings call struck an optimistic tone as management highlighted a sharp operational inflection. Revenue more than doubled, margins swung strongly into positive territory, and engagement indicators surged, giving credibility to ambitious 2026 profitability targets despite ongoing losses and funding sensitivities.

Revenue Surge Builds Confidence in Scale

Total revenue jumped 110% year over year to $39.2 million, beating guidance by $5.2 million and underscoring rapid platform scaling. The outperformance reinforces management’s confidence in reaching roughly $70 million of revenue in 2026 as monetization spreads beyond the limited 2025 city footprint.

Gross Margin Swings From Red to Robust

Marti delivered a dramatic gross margin turnaround, moving from a 15.5% loss in 2024 to a 61.1% margin in 2025 and lifting gross profit by $26.9 million. Management attributed the shift to platform monetization, operating leverage and efficiency initiatives, signaling a structurally stronger business model.

Adjusted EBITDA Loss Narrows but Remains a Drag

Adjusted EBITDA loss improved by 37% to negative $12.1 million, beating guidance by $4.9 million and showcasing better cost control and monetization. Still, the company remains in the red and is aiming for a $1 million positive adjusted EBITDA in 2026, implying a sizable $13.1 million further turnaround.

User Growth and Engagement Accelerate

Platform activity expanded rapidly, with total trips up 60% to 50.8 million and unique consumers rising 44% to 3.1 million. Trips per user climbed 11% to 16.5, indicating stronger engagement and deeper cross-service usage across Marti’s mobility offerings.

Ride-Hailing Network Scales on Both Sides

Ride-hailing showed powerful network effects as all-time riders more than doubled to 3.4 million and have already reached about 3.8 million in early 2026. Registered drivers surged 72% to 450,000 and roughly 490,000 by early 2026, reflecting growing supply and rising liquidity in the marketplace.

Geographic Expansion Broadens Addressable Market

The company now operates in 20 cities that together represent about 80% of Turkey’s GDP, significantly expanding its economic footprint. Ride-hailing was launched in 16 additional cities and delivery started in Istanbul, boosting network density and setting the stage for future monetization.

Multiservice Adoption Lifts ARPU

Marti’s multiservice strategy is gaining traction, with 35% of car-hailing and 82% of motorcycle-hailing users originating from another company service. These multiservice consumers generate 4.4 times the trips and 3.6 times the revenue of single-service users, while many drivers are already flexing between ride-hailing and delivery.

Cost Discipline and AI Support Efficiency

Cost of revenues fell 29% to $15.3 million despite strong volume growth, helped by operational efficiencies, lower depreciation and logistics savings. Management highlighted AI-enabled tools as a contributor to faster and higher-quality software development and product improvements, reinforcing margin gains.

Capital Efficiency and Liquidity Cushion

Marti ended the year with about $8 million of cash and has drawn $13 million on a convertible note facility with another $10 million available. Management argued this liquidity is sufficient to execute near-term plans without immediate fundraising, assuming progress stays on track.

Profitability Still a Work in Progress

Despite clear improvement, the company’s adjusted EBITDA remains negative and requires further gains in pricing, utilization and cost control to reach break-even. The push to turn a $12.1 million loss into a $1 million profit by 2026 is ambitious and leaves little room for missteps.

Monetization Still Concentrated in Few Cities

Monetization remained limited in 2025, with only three cities contributing meaningfully by year-end, leaving much of the footprint underpriced. The revenue growth story now hinges on successfully rolling out monetization across additional cities while maintaining user growth and competitive positioning.

2-Wheeler Fleet Shrinkage a Short-Term Constraint

Marti’s average deployed two-wheeled electric vehicle fleet declined about 29% year over year due to decommissioning older units. This reduction could temporarily constrain capacity in two-wheeler services, although management appears focused on higher-quality utilization over sheer fleet size.

Delivery Business in Early Build-Out Phase

The delivery segment launched late in the fourth quarter and is initially focused on parcel and courier use cases rather than large-scale food or grocery. Management framed delivery as a strategic adjacency with modest near-term revenue impact but potentially valuable incremental use of the existing driver base.

G&A Shows Mixed Signals

Reported general and administrative expense fell 43% to $28.1 million, mainly because of lower share-based compensation. However, G&A excluding stock comp increased 39% to $16.8 million, reflecting higher underlying operating costs required to support the company’s rapid scale.

Take Rate Below Global Benchmarks

Management acknowledged that current take rates sit in the high single to low double digits, well below the roughly 30% global benchmark. Future revenue upside depends on Marti’s ability to raise take rates gradually without losing riders or drivers, making pricing strategy a key lever.

Macro and Regulatory Clouds on the Horizon

The company flagged macro risks such as inflation and fuel-price swings, partially cushioned by government subsidies so far. It also faces an evolving national regulatory framework that could shift competitive dynamics and potentially increase customer acquisition or driver subsidy costs.

Cash Position Leaves Limited Margin for Error

With only about $8 million in cash and reliance on remaining convertible note capacity, Marti is sensitive to any slowdown in growth or monetization. If execution falls short, the company could need to revisit funding options sooner than planned, a key risk for investors watching the path to profitability.

Guidance and Outlook Signal Confidence in Momentum

Management guided to $70 million of revenue and $1 million of positive adjusted EBITDA in 2026, building on the 2025 revenue of $39.2 million and sharply improved margins. The outlook assumes broader city monetization, modest take-rate increases, growing delivery contributions and continued rider and driver growth funded by existing cash and remaining note capacity.

Marti’s earnings call painted a picture of a platform at an inflection point, with rapid growth, stronger margins and rising engagement offset by lingering losses and funding sensitivity. For investors, the story now turns on execution: expanding monetization, nudging take rates higher and maintaining volume growth, all while steering carefully through macro and regulatory risks.

data.title: Starfighters Space Faces Lawsuit From Former Chief Executive
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Starfighters Space, Inc. ( (FJET) ) just unveiled an update.

On April 9, 2026, former CEO Richard “Rick” Svetkoff filed a lawsuit in Florida state court against Starfighters Space, Inc., its current CEO Timothy Franta, several board members and Flagship Bank, alleging breach of fiduciary duty, deceptive and unfair trade practices and improper control over corporate records and assets. The complaint seeks more than $26 million in damages, various forms of equitable and injunctive relief and the transfer of certain corporate assets, including a key domain name, reflecting a deep governance and control dispute following Svetkoff’s February 19, 2026 resignation and subsequent removals from multiple subsidiary entities through early April.

Starfighters Space denies all allegations and says an internal investigation found Svetkoff opened an unauthorized offshore bank account in the company’s name and moved about $1 million of subsidiary funds, later returned, and also identified nearly $1.9 million in allegedly unauthorized transfers to Svetkoff and a company owned by his wife. The company is preparing its legal response, evaluating potential counterclaims for conversion, misappropriation of corporate assets and breach of fiduciary duty and considering referrals to authorities, while warning that an unfavorable outcome in the litigation could materially affect its business, financial condition or operating results.

More about Starfighters Space, Inc.

Starfighters Space, Inc. operates in the space and aerospace sector through wholly owned subsidiaries including Starfighters International, Inc. and Starfighters, Inc., with banking relationships involving Flagship Bank. The company’s leadership recently transitioned, with former CEO Richard “Rick” Svetkoff resigning from all executive and board roles on February 19, 2026, and subsequently being removed from related subsidiary positions in March and April 2026.

Average Trading Volume: 1,227,435

Current Market Cap: $213.1M

For a thorough assessment of FJET stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: SharonAI Accelerates Proceeds from Data Center Stake Sale
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from SharonAI Holdings ( (SHAZ) ) is now available.

On April 13, 2026, SharonAI Holdings Inc. announced it has accelerated the receipt of the remaining consideration from the sale of its 50% stake in Texas Critical Data Centers LLC to New Era Energy & Digital, Inc., lifting total proceeds to US$74 million, above the originally expected US$70 million. The payment acceleration follows NUAI’s April 10, 2026 decision to redeem in full a US$50 million senior secured convertible promissory note ahead of its June 30, 2026 maturity, coupled with a true-up share issuance tied to NUAI’s recent equity offering.

The transaction delivers US$10 million in previously received cash plus the early redemption proceeds and roughly 893,724 additional NUAI shares, with Sharon AI retaining the option to convert up to 20% of the note into NUAI stock by April 17, 2026 and the remainder payable in cash by April 24, 2026. Management highlighted that the non-dilutive inflow strengthens Sharon AI’s balance sheet and financial flexibility, supporting its strategy to scale sovereign AI and high-performance cloud infrastructure in Australia and reinforcing its partnership with NUAI, with implications for enhanced capacity deployment and customer reach in the Asia-Pacific AI infrastructure market.

The most recent analyst rating on (SHAZ) stock is a Buy with a $40.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on SharonAI Holdings stock, see the SHAZ Stock Forecast page.

More about SharonAI Holdings

SharonAI Holdings Inc., listed on Nasdaq as SHAZ, is a leading Australian neocloud and high-performance computing company focused on artificial intelligence and cloud GPU compute infrastructure. Its cloud GPU platform supports AI factories and sovereign AI solutions, targeting hyperscale, enterprise, AI-native, research and government customers across the Asia-Pacific region.

The company concentrates on delivering advanced GPU capacity and high-performance AI solutions through partnerships and an expanding data center footprint. By scaling sovereign AI and HPC cloud infrastructure in Australia, Sharon AI aims to power the next wave of accelerated computing adoption while creating sustained value for shareholders in its core markets.

The latest announcement is out from Boundless Bio Inc. ( (BOLD) ).

On April 13, 2026, Boundless Bio, Inc. agreed to terminate its lease for approximately 80,168 square feet of laboratory and office space at 10955 Alexandria Way in San Diego, a lease that had originally been scheduled to run through October 31, 2034. Under the termination agreement with landlord ARE-10933 North Torrey Pines, LLC, the lease will end effective May 31, 2026, and the deal became effective once the landlord secured a replacement tenant for the premises.

As part of the arrangement, Boundless Bio will pay a $10 million lease modification fee and forfeit its roughly $0.5 million security deposit, representing a meaningful cash outlay to exit the long-term commitment. The move alters the company’s future facilities footprint and long-term lease obligations, with financial and operational implications for how it houses its laboratory and office functions going forward.

data.title: Sify Technologies Posts FY 2025-26 Revenue Growth but Books Loss as Data Center Expansion Accelerates
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Sify Technologies ( (SIFY) ).

On April 13, 2026, Sify Technologies reported its unaudited consolidated IFRS results for fiscal 2025-26, posting revenue of INR 44,877 million, up 13% year-on-year, and EBITDA of INR 9,871 million, a 31% increase. Despite operational improvement, the company recorded a loss after tax of INR 1,366 million, reflecting higher depreciation, finance costs and recognition of additional coupon expense linked to the February 2026 CCD-to-equity conversion under its agreement with Kotak.

Management highlighted continued investment in data centers, network expansion and talent, with FY 2025-26 capex reaching INR 13,282 million and year-end cash at INR 5,071 million. Network and data center services each contributed 39% of revenue and grew 12% and 23% respectively, while digital services declined slightly, and Sify’s data center arm sold 17MW during the year and contracted an additional 81MW for FY 2026-27, underpinning its positioning to benefit from India’s accelerating digital infrastructure build-out and the forthcoming IPO of its data center subsidiary, for which SEBI has issued final observations.

Sify also reported strong customer wins across telecom, financial services, insurance, cloud and public sector clients, including large network integration deals, new data center and colocation contracts from global hyperscalers and internet players, and expansion of cloud and managed services engagements in India and North America. Management framed these developments, along with supportive policy measures such as tax incentives for foreign cloud players using Indian data centers, as reinforcing Sify’s strategic role in India’s evolving digital ecosystem while it maintains a focus on cost competitiveness, cash flow optimization and fiscal discipline.

Spark’s Take on SIFY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SIFY is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held back by weak financial performance (net losses, rising leverage, and negative free cash flow from heavy capex). The earnings call provides a meaningful offset due to strong Data Center/Network momentum and contracted backlog, while technicals are neutral-to-soft and valuation is constrained by unprofitability and no dividend data.

To see Spark’s full report on SIFY stock, click here.

More about Sify Technologies

Sify Technologies Limited is an Indian information and communications technology company that operates across network services, data center services and digital services. The Chennai-headquartered firm focuses on enterprise connectivity, cloud and data center infrastructure, and digital transformation solutions for domestic and increasingly global customers.

Average Trading Volume: 69,683

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: $994.5M

Learn more about SIFY stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

ProText Mobility ( (TXTM) ) has provided an update.

On March 13, 2026, ProText Mobility issued a comprehensive strategic update outlining discretionary stock purchases by its president and chairman, who has only been accumulating TXTM shares, as well as its continued avoidance of executive salaries, debt, and reverse stock splits to support shareholder value. The company detailed liquidity arrangements with BNY Mellon, Standard Bank, Absa, and Melville Douglas via custodial wallets, alongside a custodial wallet pilot program aimed at supporting foreign direct trade and the influx of foreign equities.

ProText highlighted its use of a blockchain-enabled RWA tokenization platform, combined with proprietary kettle tech and nano-technology, to improve bioavailability of active pharmaceutical ingredients and unlock new tokenized asset revenue streams. It also emphasized its HULE reinvestment strategy, the expected balance-sheet uplift from seed stock and the TruLeaf LOI under IFRS standards, and progress toward IFRS-audited financials and a Deloitte valuation as stepping stones to a planned uplisting to a major exchange.

Additional initiatives disclosed included development of a new corporate website with an integrated investor relations suite, voluntary use of the SEC’s EDGAR system to enhance disclosure transparency, and positioning to benefit from favorable USD/ZAR foreign exchange movements. ProText further underscored its ESG and sustainability focus, including integrating carbon credits into its tokenization system and pursuing a zero-waste, environmentally aligned business model to support long-term growth and shareholder value.

More about ProText Mobility

ProText Mobility Inc. is a biotech company focused on research, testing, and development of highly bioavailable botanical formulations for nutraceutical and pharmaceutical applications. Leveraging proprietary live-plant extraction, nano-technology, and “kettle tech,” it also positions itself as a leader in blockchain-enabled real-world asset tokenization and compliant RWA solutions targeting global health and wellness markets.

The company integrates its biotech assets with tokenization platforms to create digital representations of active pharmaceutical ingredients and other real-world assets, aiming to enhance liquidity and capital flexibility. Its strategy emphasizes zero debt, financial discipline without executive salaries, and protection against shareholder dilution, while pursuing an uplisting to a major securities exchange and broader institutional investor access.

Listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker BMNR, the company focuses on holding ETH as its primary treasury reserve asset and operates MAVAN, its own Made-in-America validator network for institutional-grade Ethereum staking.

data.title: Mirion Grants CEO Large Performance-Based Stock Option Award
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Mirion Technologies ( (MIR) ).

On April 9, 2026, Mirion Technologies’ board approved a one-time grant of 2.5 million performance-vesting stock options to founder, chairman and CEO Thomas Logan, structured to vest over three- and four-year performance periods based on relative total shareholder return against the Russell 2000 ex-financials and his continued service. The options, which can pay out from 0% to 150% of target and require stock price appreciation and at least 60th percentile TSR to deliver any value, are intended to retain Logan, tightly align his incentives with shareholders over a five-year horizon, and will generate non-cash charges reflected in future earnings guidance.

The award includes a one-year post-vesting holding period, limited prorated vesting if Logan departs after the third anniversary other than for cause, and specific protections in a change-of-control scenario where earned options may vest if his service ends without cause or for good reason. The compensation committee positions this as a singular, extraordinary equity grant outside the normal pay cycle, emphasizing that, combined with his regular long-term incentives, all of Logan’s equity compensation is now performance-contingent and aimed at driving sustained market outperformance.

The most recent analyst rating on (MIR) stock is a Buy with a $28.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Mirion Technologies stock, see the MIR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on MIR Stock

RenX Enterprises ( (RENX) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On April 10, 2026, RenX Enterprises Corp. received written notice from Nasdaq that it had regained compliance with the exchange’s minimum bid price requirement, after its common stock closed at or above $1.00 for 10 consecutive business days from March 26 through April 9, 2026. The company disclosed on April 13, 2026, that the compliance matter is now closed and its shares will continue trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol RENX, removing an overhang on the stock’s listing status and providing stability for investors as management pursues its 2026 operational initiatives.

Chief executive David Villarreal said operations are performing well, the commercial pipeline remains active, and the company is progressing in line with its 2026 priorities, underscoring that the Nasdaq resolution allows RenX to stay focused on executing its strategy. The development helps solidify RenX’s capital markets footing at a time when it is aiming to leverage its advanced processing technologies and regionalized production model to move further into higher-value engineered growing media markets, which could have implications for both growth prospects and shareholder value if operational momentum continues.

Stardust Power ( (SDST) ) has provided an announcement.

On April 13, 2026, Stardust Power Inc. announced it had signed a non-binding Letter of Intent with a strategic counterparty to secure up to 15,000 metric tons per year of lithium carbonate equivalent in the form of lithium chloride from a California lithium brine project. Initial deliveries are contemplated for the first half of 2028 to the company’s Muskogee, Oklahoma refinery, with an option for Stardust Power to purchase additional volumes at its discretion.

The prospective agreement forms part of Stardust Power’s broader strategy to build a diversified, scalable U.S.-based feedstock pipeline in support of its Muskogee refinery as it advances toward construction and commissioning. With multiple feedstock arrangements underway and key project milestones such as completion of FEL-3 engineering and receipt of an air quality construction permit, the company aims to bolster long-term operational readiness and reinforce its positioning in domestic lithium refining, although the LOI remains subject to due diligence and a definitive agreement that is not guaranteed to be executed.

On April 13, 2026, Palvella Therapeutics increased the size of its board of directors from six to seven members and appointed dermatologist and healthcare investment analyst John Doux, M.D., as a Class III director, with his initial term set to expire at the 2026 annual meeting of stockholders. Doux, who previously served on Palvella’s board before its reverse merger and has extensive experience in rare disease therapeutics, dermatology innovation forums, and biotech governance, was granted a standard non-employee director equity award of 6,000 stock options vesting over 36 months, and the board determined he qualifies as an independent director under Nasdaq rules.

The appointment of Dr. Doux, who combines clinical dermatology practice, healthcare-focused investment experience, and prior board roles at rare disease and dermatology-focused companies, is expected to deepen Palvella’s strategic and scientific oversight in its core therapeutic areas. His independence status and lack of related-party ties reinforce the company’s corporate governance framework, while his background in advancing novel treatments for rare skin conditions aligns closely with Palvella’s focus and may strengthen its positioning with investors and partners in the rare disease and dermatology segments.

Conagra Brands ( (CAG) ) has issued an update.

Conagra Brands announced on April 13, 2026, that veteran consumer goods executive John Brase will become President and Chief Executive Officer and join the board, effective June 1, 2026, succeeding long-time leader Sean Connolly, who will step down from his CEO and director roles on May 31, 2026. Brase, formerly President and COO of J.M. Smucker and a 30-year Procter & Gamble executive, will receive an at-will employment package that includes a $1.15 million base salary for fiscal 2027, performance-linked bonus and equity incentives, relocation support and a double-trigger change-in-control agreement.

The carefully sequenced leadership transition underscores Conagra’s succession planning and signals continuity in its strategy of driving profitable growth in branded foods, particularly in frozen and snacks, following Connolly’s more than decade-long effort to streamline the portfolio and navigate challenges from the pandemic, inflation and supply chain disruptions. With Brase’s track record in portfolio management, brand building and operational execution, the board is positioning Conagra to build on recent gains in revenue growth, margins and cash flow, aiming to further enhance value for investors, customers, employees and other stakeholders as Connolly exits with separation benefits governed by his existing agreement.

Conagra Brands, Inc. is one of North America’s leading branded food companies, combining a century-long heritage with a focus on innovation across frozen, snacks and other packaged food categories.
